articles.title: UP basic schools closed till June 24: Classes to resume on June 25 amid heatwave conditions - Bhaskar English

articles.full_text:
UP basic schools closed till June 24:Classes to resume on June 25 amid heatwave conditions
The Uttar Pradesh government has extended the summer vacation period in Basic Education Council schools in view of the severe heat and heat-wave. According to the order issued by Additional Chief Secretary Basic Education Parth Sarathi Sen Sharma, now in council and recognized schools, summer vacation will be from May 20 to June 24 every year. Earlier, this vacation was fixed till June 15.
Under the new arrangement, all basic schools in the state will open for regular teaching-learning from June 25. However, teachers, education assistants, instructors and non-teaching staff will have to be present in school on June 22, 23 and 24 to complete necessary arrangements.
These arrangements must be made before schools open as per the order
The order has given instructions to ensure cleanliness of the premises, kitchen and toilets before schools open. Besides this, it has been asked to ensure preparation of mid-day meals, distribution of textbooks, preparation of lesson plans, fixing electricity and drinking water arrangements, making Bal Vatika, smart classes and ICT labs operational, and ensuring availability of sports equipment.
Yoga Day will be celebrated on 21 June
The government has also emphasized on organizing meetings of the School Education Committee and taking community support. On 21 June, on the occasion of International Yoga Day, teachers and students will practice yoga collectively in schools.
220 teaching says mandatory
The Additional Chief Secretary has directed all District Magistrates and Divisional Commissioners to ensure compliance with the provisions of the Right to Education (RTE) Act before declaring additional holidays at the local level. According to the Act, it is mandatory to have at least 220 teaching days during an academic session in primary level schools.
On June 21, on the occasion of International Yoga Day, teachers and students in schools will perform collective yoga practice.
This decision by the government will provide relief from heat to lakhs of students in the state, while school administration will also get additional time for preparations for the new academic session.
